title: Understanding the Forces
text: Andrea's force is directed due north, while Jennifer's force is at an angle of $$32$$ degrees east of north. We need to find the resultant force by combining these two forces.

title: Breaking Down Jennifer's Force
text: Jennifer's force can be broken down into two components: one in the north direction and one in the east direction. Use trigonometric functions to find these components.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: To find the north component of Jennifer's force, use the cosine function: $$180cos\left(32\right)$$. For the east component, use the sine function: $$180sin\left(32\right)$$.

title: Combining the Forces
text: Add Andrea's force to the north component of Jennifer's force to get the total northward force. The eastward force is just Jennifer's east component.

title: Finding the Resultant Force
text: Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the magnitude of the resultant force: sqrt((north component)**2+(east component)**2).

title: Calculate the Magnitude
text: Calculate the magnitude of the resultant force using the components you found.
